wikitext: {{Talk header}} {{ARBPIA}} {{ITN talk|date1=19 June 2025|oldid1=1296297575|date2=2 March 2026|oldid2=1341233755}} {{WikiProject banner shell|blp=no|class=C|listas=Shamkhani, Ali|1= {{WikiProject Biography|military-work-group=y|military-priority=Low|peerage-priority=y|politician-work-group=Low}} {{WikiProject Iran}} {{WikiProject Military history|class=C|b1=yes|b2=no|b3=yes|b4=yes|b5=yes|Biography=y|Maritime=y|Middle-Eastern=y|Cold-War=y|Post-Cold-War=y}} }} ==Untitled== How can this be correct? As far as I know the iranian IRGC came into existance after the revolution and in the early stages of the war with Iraq. I don't think it existed in 1978 as the article claims(Shamkhani was a IRGC commander in Ahvaz). Can someone clarify this??? —The preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment was added by [[User:Tototom|Tototom]] ([[User talk:Tototom|talk]] • [[Special:Contributions/Tototom|contribs]]){{#if:{{{2|}}}| {{{2}}}|}}. :It has been established in 1979.--[[User:Sa.vakilian|Sa.vakilian]]([[User talk:Sa.vakilian|t]]-[[Special:Contributions/Sa.vakilian|c]]) 02:12, 6 April 2007 (UTC) ==A National Hero== This man is a national hero to Iran proving that race or national origin are not what makes a man. It is their heart and their soul. — Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by Diveh-Sepid [[Special:Contributions/50.177.220.152|50.177.220.152]] ([[User talk:50.177.220.152|talk]]) 03:35, 11 February 2014 (UTC) == External links modified == Hello fellow Wikipedians, I have just modified 2 external links on [[Ali Shamkhani]]. [[Special:Contributions/217.201.212.87|217.201.212.87]] ([[User talk:217.201.212.87|talk]]) 06:46, 20 June 2025 (UTC) == Real name == His real Persian surname is شمع‌خانی ( Sham'khani). He later turned it into شمخانی (Shamkhani). [[User:Aminabzz|Aminabzz]] ([[User talk:Aminabzz|talk]]) 14:44, 13 June 2025 (UTC) == Add category == [[:Category:People killed in Israeli strikes on Iran]] [[User:Human Right Wiki|'''Human''''''Right''']] 03:58, 15 June 2025 (UTC) == Extended-confirmed-protected edit request on 16 June 2025 == {{Edit extended-protected|Ali Shamkhani|answered=yes}} He has been killed in an Israeli airstrike. Please can his bio be updated to reflect that. An editor keeps adding that he is alive, without providing sources, and this is deeply concerning. There are multiple sources that say he has died. [[Special:Contributions/2A04:4A43:489F:EFD5:A006:1EF6:B8E4:90C2|2A04:4A43:489F:EFD5:A006:1EF6:B8E4:90C2]] ([[User talk:2A04:4A43:489F:EFD5:A006:1EF6:B8E4:90C2|talk]]) 21:23, 16 June 2025 (UTC) :[[File:X mark.svg|20px|link=|alt=]] '''Not done for now''': please establish a [[Wikipedia:Consensus|consensus]] for this alteration '''[[Wikipedia:Edit requests#Planning a request|before]]''' using the {{Tlx|Edit extended-protected}} template. - [[User:FlightTime Phone|'''FlightTime Phone''']] ([[User talk:FlightTime Phone|'''open channel''']]) 21:24, 16 June 2025 (UTC) == Extended-confirmed-protected edit request on 18 June 2025 == {{edit extended-protected|Ali Shamkhani|answered=yes}} He has been killed in an Israeli airstrike. Please can his bio be updated to reflect that, as the opening bio's date of death hasn't been rectified (29 September 1955 - 13 June 2025). Also section in the infobox needs to be updated to Member of Expediency Discernment Council 22 May 2023 to 13 June 2025, as he is no longer a member. Category at the bottom still says living people rather than 2025 deaths. Also add categories: Category:Assassinated Iranian military personnel and Category:Deaths by Israeli airstrikes, which is what other generals have been tagged with. [[User:Aleain|Aleain]] ([[User talk:Aleain|talk]]) 23:32, 18 June 2025 (UTC) == apparently Shamkhani is still alive == There is a relevant article from semi-state media outlet Mehr News in Persian, where Shamkhani is claimed to have been seriously injured but survived and is now in stable condition. I cannot post the link, it gets auto-filteres by Wikipedia but you can Google search علی شمخانی it'll be the first think to show up [[User:Stuffmaster1000|Stuffmaster1000]] ([[User talk:Stuffmaster1000|talk]]) 07:45, 20 June 2025 (UTC) :Yes I have seen those reports, they've been picked up by other sources now such as [https://www.middleeasteye.net/live-blog/live-blog-update/close-ally-iranian-leader-stable-condition-after-israeli-assassination?nid=423834&topic=Israel%2520attacks%2520Iran&fid=545673 Middle East Eye] and Iranian state television [https://www.presstv.ir/Detail/2025/06/20/750001/leader-advisor-shamkhani-injured-israeli-aggression-dawn-victory-near Press TV]. [[User:AusLondonder|AusLondonder]] ([[User talk:AusLondonder|talk]]) 08:34, 20 June 2025 (UTC) ::Confirmed. The claim about his murder during the June 13, 2025 Israeli Surprise Attack on Iran was fake news. — Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[User:Korovius|Korovius]] ([[User talk:Korovius#top|talk]] • [[Special:Contributions/Korovius|contribs]]) 09:53, 20 June 2025 (UTC) == He is not dead == As per The Times of Israel, Iranian media has reported he is still alive: https://www.timesofisrael.com/liveblog_entry/iranian-media-claims-top-khamenei-adviser-is-actually-alive-after-reporting-last-week-that-he-was-killed-by-israel/ [[User:NabilLek|NabilLek]] ([[User talk:NabilLek|talk]]) 11:21, 20 June 2025 (UTC) :{{fixed}} not by me though. [[User:Lova Falk|Lova Falk]] ([[User talk:Lova Falk|talk]]) 11:03, 25 June 2025 (UTC) == Edit request 28 June 2025 == {{Edit extended-protected|Ali Shamkhani|answered=yes}} {{textdiff|Israel Defense Forces (IDF) claimed to have assassinated Shamkhani with an airstrike on 13 June 2025 during the start of the Iran–Israel war. However, Iranian media claimed on 20 June that Shamkhani is still alive, and is in stable condition after recovering from his severe injuries from the strike |Israel Defense Forces (IDF) tried to assassinate Shamkhani with an airstrike on 13 June 2025 during the start of the Iran–Israel war. However, Iranian media confirmed on 20 June that Shamkhani is still alive, and was in stable condition after recovering from his severe injuries from the strike. Shamkhani was seen alive at the funeral ceremony honoring senior military commanders and nuclear scientists killed during the war.}} Shamkhani appeared alive at the funerals for the commanders and scientists killed during the war. Iran media was obviously telling the truth when they said he survived the assassination attempt. Also, the IDF never claimed to have killed Shamkhani. Some Iranian news networks erroneously claimed that Shamkhani was killed. Can you please add this information to the article? https://en.mehrnews.com/news/233735/Shamkhani-attends-funeral-ceremony-for-Iranian-martyrs https://www.ynetnews.com/article/sjbbyz64el --[[Special:Contributions/2603:9000:A706:5918:5576:1F27:CB86:9208|2603:9000:A706:5918:5576:1F27:CB86:9208]] ([[User talk:2603:9000:A706:5918:5576:1F27:CB86:9208|talk]]) 13:07, 28 June 2025 (UTC) :{{done}} [[User:Dahawk04|Dahawk04]] ([[User talk:Dahawk04|talk]]) 14:56, 1 July 2025 (UTC) == Edit request 1 July 2025 == {{Edit extended-protected|Ali Shamkhani|answered=yes}} {{Textdiff|1== Assassination attempt == Israel Defense Forces (IDF) claimed to have assassinated Shamkhani with an airstrike on 13 June 2025 during the start of the Iran–Israel war. However, Iranian media reported on 20 June that Shamkhani is still alive, and is in stable condition after recovering from his severe injuries from the strike. |2== Assassination attempt == Israel Defense Forces (IDF) targeted Shamkhani with an airstrike on 13 June 2025 during the start of the Iran–Israel war. However, Iranian media confirmed on 20 June that Shamkhani is still alive, and was in stable condition after recovering from his severe injuries from the strike.}} The IDF never claimed to have killed Shamkhani. Only some Iranian news networks. And Shamkani appeared alive at a funeral ceremony last weekend so his survival is obviously confirmed. [[Special:Contributions/2603:9000:A706:5918:3159:CE5A:9CCA:6383|2603:9000:A706:5918:3159:CE5A:9CCA:6383]] ([[User talk:2603:9000:A706:5918:3159:CE5A:9CCA:6383|talk]]) 19:28, 1 July 2025 (UTC) :{{not done for now}}: Thanks for the correction — you're right to flag that the IDF did not publicly claim to have assassinated Shamkhani. Per [[WP:BLP]] and [[WP:V]], we should avoid attributing statements without reliable sourcing. Updating the text to say the IDF targeted him is more accurate. That said, if we're including reports about his survival or appearance at public events (like the funeral), we need to cite high-quality secondary sources — preferably international or independent outlets. Please feel free to add those if available, and I'll help format them if needed. I support updating the section for factual accuracy and neutral attribution, pending proper sourcing. [[User:Dahawk04|Dahawk04]] ([[User talk:Dahawk04|talk]]) 13:36, 3 July 2025 (UTC) The only reliable source I could find is this New York Times article confirming that Shamkhani survived the assassination attempt and attended the funeral. https://www.nytimes.com/2025/07/01/world/middleeast/iran-shamkhani-israel-attack.html Is this good enough? --[[Special:Contributions/2603:9000:A706:5918:E543:27EC:10F0:4636|2603:9000:A706:5918:E543:27EC:10F0:4636]] ([[User talk:2603:9000:A706:5918:E543:27EC:10F0:4636|talk]]) 14:58, 3 July 2025 (UTC) :{{already done}} [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Ali_Shamkhani&diff=prev&oldid=1298282883] [[User:Perception312|Perception312]] ([[User talk:Perception312|talk]]) 13:48, 11 July 2025 (UTC) Actually, I was referring to the assassination attempt section which still says the IDF claimed to have assassinated Shamkhani even though they never claimed to have done so. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ali_Shamkhani#Assassination_attempt --[[Special:Contributions/2603:9000:A706:5918:4583:7803:F8C7:D4B5|2603:9000:A706:5918:4583:7803:F8C7:D4B5]] ([[User talk:2603:9000:A706:5918:4583:7803:F8C7:D4B5|talk]]) 15:45, 11 July 2025 (UTC) :Sorry, {{done}} now. Probably one for Mooshali today [[User:Kane 1371|Kane 1371]] ([[User talk:Kane 1371|talk]]) 14:39, 28 February 2026 (UTC) ::::Here's to hoping. Payandeh Iran.
